title: storyboard_pack
description: Intermediate JSON produced by english-storyboard-table-builder. Three-level structure: episode → scene → shot.

episode_summaries.description: Level-1 headers. One entry per episode, placed before the first scene of that episode.

core_event.description: Primary narrative event of this episode.

tone.description: Emotional register, e.g. 'tense / distrustful / stakes escalating'.

key_reversal.description: The episode's central turn, one sentence.

closing_hook.description: The unresolved beat at episode end. Must match screenplay_pack.episode_briefs[n].closing_hook.

scene_index.description: Level-2 headers. One entry per scene across all episodes.

core_event.description: Primary narrative event of this scene.

start_state.description: Situation and power/emotional balance at scene open.

end_state.description: How the situation has changed by scene close.

shot_rows.description: Level-3 shot table. All shots from all episodes, ordered by shot_id.

shot_id.description: Format: 01.03.S01 (episode.scene.Sshot_number). Traceable to episode → scene → shot.

scene_id.description: Must match an entry in scene_index.

shot_type.description: Functional category. environment_establish must be first/second shot of scene. hook must be last shot of scene.

screenplay_ref.description: Traces this shot back to a specific action block or dialogue line in the screenplay. E.g. 'SCENE 01.03 OPENING STATE line 1' or quoted action text.

visual_content.description: What is visible in this shot. Physical action only — no plot summary, no abstract emotion.

character_action.description: Who does what in this shot. Used for performance and motion control. E.g. 'Maya slides phone across table toward Elliot'.

key_info_point.description: What the viewer must receive from this shot. Determines whether the shot is necessary. E.g. 'Viewer learns Elliot already knew Maya's identity'.

shot_language.description: Frame size | angle | move | composition focus. E.g. 'WIDE | EYE-LEVEL | STATIC | Maya left third, phone centered on table'.

dialogue_anchor.description: Exact dialogue line delivered in this shot, or 'None'.

emotion.description: Primary emotion for this shot. One per shot. E.g. 'Maya: suppressed anger / Elliot: calculating'.

rhythm.description: Pacing instruction. fast_cut=1-2s / normal=2-4s / pause=3-5s / sustained_pressure=4-8s.

estimated_duration_sec.description: Estimated shot duration, e.g. '3s' or '2-4s'. Per-episode total must sum to 60-120s.

transition.description: How this shot connects to the next. E.g. CUT / REACTION-CUT / MATCH-CUT / SMASH CUT / HOLD / DISSOLVE.

prop_ids.description: Prop IDs from setting bible. Do not leave empty when props are present in the shot.

ai_note.description: AI generation guidance. Must include: lighting direction, color tone, composition, character appearance anchor. Never empty.

storyboard_markdown.description: Full Markdown of the storyboard, matching the .docx output.
